name: web-runtime-baseline
description: Web Runtime: configuration platform for the experience-frame (chrome). Tenants register zone replacements (swap the platform header for their own), slot replacements (replace one component inside a zone), menus, and entitlements bound to audience segments. Defaults to Web SDK v3 as the host SDK; the SDK's `runtime` module is the consumer surface.

attributes.summary: Experience-frame configuration: zones, slots, menus, entitlements, audience segmentation. Consumed by web apps via the Web SDK's `runtime` module (v3+).

host_sdk.description: Web Runtime ships configured to be consumed via Web SDK v3+. Apps on Web SDK v2 cannot reach the runtime config endpoints — they'll receive HTTP 406 with the upgrade hint.

endpoints.summary: Return the resolved runtime config for the calling user + tenant. Resolution: platform defaults, then tenant overrides, then audience-segment-conditional overrides matched against the caller's identity claims.
List all zones registered for this tenant.
Register or replace a zone wholesale. Requires `admin` role. zone_name must be in the platform's named-zone set: 'header', 'sidebar', 'content-frame', 'footer'.

kind.description: 'full' means the tenant supplies a whole React component to render instead of the platform default. 'composite' means the platform shell renders + the tenant slots in components per slot (see /slots).

component_ref.description: Module-federation reference, e.g. 'tenant-acme/HeaderShell' or a CDN URL. Must be served from a tenant-trusted origin.

audience_segment.description: Apply only when caller matches the named audience. Omit for org-wide.

endpoints.summary: Replace ONE slot inside a zone. Use when you want to keep the platform shell for a zone but swap a specific component inside (e.g. replace the default search with a tenant-supplied search). Slot names per zone are platform-defined; see /v1/runtime/slots.
List slot names available in each platform-defined zone. Useful for the admin UI to surface what's replaceable.
Revert a zone to the platform default. Idempotent.
List all menus this tenant has registered.
Register or replace a menu. Menus are trees of items; each item has an audience-segment + entitlement gate so the rendered menu adapts per user.

anchor.description: Where this menu attaches in the shell.

action.description: Optional client-side action handler name. Mutually exclusive with `href`.

audience_segment.description: Only render this item when caller matches.

required_entitlement.description: Only render when caller has this entitlement.

endpoints.summary: List audience segments registered for this tenant.
Define an audience segment. Segments are evaluated against identity claims + preferences at config-resolution time.

match.description: Boolean expression over the caller's identity claims + entitlements. Example: `{all: [{claim: 'roles', contains: 'trader'}, {claim: 'tenant_id', equals: '...'}]}`. Supports `all` / `any` / `not` / leaf predicates (`equals`, `contains`, `in`, `matches`).

endpoints.summary: List entitlements registered for this tenant + the audience segments that grant them.
Register an entitlement and the audiences it's granted to.

named_zones.name: header
named_zones.description: Top app bar. Hosts branding, search, user menu, notifications bell, help.

named_zones.name: sidebar
named_zones.description: Primary navigation rail. Tenant extensions can append items below the platform primary-nav.

named_zones.name: content-frame
named_zones.description: Page-content wrapper. Hosts page title, breadcrumbs, actions toolbar above the route's component tree.

named_zones.name: footer
named_zones.description: Bottom bar with legal links, build version, support link.

replacement_semantics.full_zone: Tenant supplies the entire zone's React component tree. The platform shell renders the tenant's component as the zone — no platform UI in that zone. Use when the tenant needs full visual control (e.g. a custom-branded header that doesn't share Salt tokens).
replacement_semantics.slot_replacement: Tenant supplies ONE component for ONE slot within an otherwise-platform-rendered zone. Platform owns layout, density, and the surrounding chrome; tenant owns just that slot's render. Most common for `header.search` (replace platform search with a tenant component that knows tenant-specific entities).
replacement_semantics.audience_conditional: Both zone and slot replacements can be conditioned on an audience-segment match. Resolution at request time: evaluate every replacement's `audience_segment` against caller identity; most-specific match wins, ties broken by registration recency.

leaf_predicates.name: contains
leaf_predicates.description: Caller's claim is an array; contains the given value.

facts.title: Overview
facts.body: Web Runtime is the configuration platform that drives every web app's experience frame. Tenants register zone replacements (swap the platform header for their own), slot replacements (swap one component inside a zone — e.g. replace search), menus, and entitlements bound to audience segments. The runtime evaluates audience-match expressions against each caller's identity claims + entitlements; the resolved config is what Web SDK v3+'s `runtime` module returns to the app at boot. Web apps consume this exclusively through the Web SDK — there is no direct browser-to-Web-Runtime call path.

facts.title: Registering a full-zone replacement
Slot replacements vs full-zone replacements
Audience segmentation + entitlements
Menus that adapt per user
Security model
facts.body: Component refs (the `component_ref` field on zone + slot replacements) are gated by a per-tenant trusted-origin allow-list, configured at tenant onboarding. The platform shell rejects refs from non-allow-listed origins at load time. Audience-match expressions are evaluated server-side at config-resolution time; clients NEVER receive the match grammar, only the resolved entitlement list — so an attacker inspecting client traffic can't learn the segmentation rules. Entitlement checks via `webSdk.runtime.hasEntitlement` are advisory on the client (UX); every server endpoint MUST also enforce its own entitlement check via the Identity claim set — the client-side check is for hiding affordances, not authorization.

properties.reason: Web apps consume Web Runtime exclusively through the Web SDK's `runtime` module. Web Runtime ships configured to require Web SDK v3.0+ as the host SDK.
Audience-match expressions are evaluated against the caller's identity claims (roles, scopes, org_unit, …). Entitlement grants resolve through identity-claim membership.
User-driven runtime preferences (density, theme) feed into the resolved config alongside tenant configuration.
